pi_items.verified_value: Brown v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954)
pi_items.source: Library of Congress, Justia U.S. Supreme Court Center, Cornell Legal Information Institute - all confirm landmark 1954 SCOTUS decision on equal protection and racial segregation in schools
Library of Congress, Justia U.S. Supreme Court Center, Cornell Legal Information Institute - all confirm landmark 1966 SCOTUS decision on custodial interrogation and Fifth Amendment rights
Library of Congress, Justia U.S. Supreme Court Center, Cornell Legal Information Institute, U.S. Courts - all confirm landmark 1963 SCOTUS decision on right to counsel
Task description explicitly requires this exact text as part of note content

Brown v. Board of Education decision explicitly based on Equal Protection Clause of Fourteenth Amendment. Web search confirmed phrase is central to the case holding.
Miranda v. Arizona case explicitly requires procedural safeguards for custodial interrogation. U.S. Courts and Cornell LII confirmed this as core holding of the case.
Gideon v. Wainwright decision establishes the right to counsel as fundamental right under Sixth Amendment. U.S. Courts and Cornell LII confirmed this as core holding of the case.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a standalone note in Jurism (legal reference management software) containing text about three landmark U.S. Supreme Court cases: Brown v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954), which established that segregation violates the Equal Protection Clause; Miranda v. Arizona, 384 U.S. 436 (1966), which established Fifth Amendment protections during custodial interrogation; and Gideon v. Wainwright, 372 U.S. 335 (1963), which established the Sixth Amendment right to counsel in state proceedings. The note title 'Research Memo: Constitutional Rights Framework' and all bonus phrases (equal protection, custodial interrogation, right to counsel) directly correspond to verified legal doctrine from these three cases. The Jurism database is pre-populated with real legal case references via database injection during task setup.
